stem: Use Lagrange multipliers to find the minimum value of \( f(x,y) = [[A]]x^{2} + [[B]]y^{2} \) subject to the constraint \( [[p]]x + [[q]]y = [[r]] \).
solution: The Lagrange conditions are \( 2[[A]]x=\lambda [[p]] \), \( 2[[B]]y=\lambda [[q]] \), together with the constraint \( [[p]]x+[[q]]y=[[r]] \). Solving this system for \( x,y,\lambda \) and substituting back into \( f \) gives the minimum value \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the unconstrained critical value of \( f \) at the origin, ignoring the constraint entirely.
This swaps which weight (\( [[A]] \) or \( [[B]] \)) pairs with which constraint coefficient squared.
This ignores the weights \( [[A]],[[B]] \) entirely, as if minimizing \( x^2+y^2 \).
This naively sets \( x=0 \) and solves the constraint for \( y \), rather than actually minimizing.
This is off by two from the correct minimum value.
This is off by two from the correct minimum value.

stem: Use Lagrange multipliers to find the extreme value of \( f(x,y) = xy \) subject to the constraint \( [[a]]x + [[b]]y = [[c]] \).
solution: The Lagrange conditions are \( y=\lambda [[a]] \), \( x=\lambda [[b]] \), together with the constraint \( [[a]]x+[[b]]y=[[c]] \). Solving this system for \( x,y,\lambda \) and substituting back into \( f=xy \) gives \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( f \) evaluated at the origin, ignoring the constraint entirely.
This drops the factor of 4 that arises from solving the Lagrange system.
This drops a factor of 2 that arises from solving the Lagrange system.
This mistakenly uses \( [[a]] \) for both coefficients instead of correctly pairing \( [[a]] \) with \( x \) and \( [[b]] \) with \( y \).
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.

stem: Use Lagrange multipliers to find the minimum value of \( f(x,y) = [[A]]x^{2} + [[B]]xy + [[C]]y^{2} \) subject to the constraint \( [[p]]x + [[q]]y = [[r]] \).
solution: The Lagrange conditions are \( 2[[A]]x+[[B]]y=\lambda [[p]] \), \( [[B]]x+2[[C]]y=\lambda [[q]] \), together with the constraint \( [[p]]x+[[q]]y=[[r]] \). Solving this system for \( x,y,\lambda \) and substituting back into \( f \) gives the minimum value \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the unconstrained critical value of \( f \) at the origin, ignoring the constraint entirely.
This has the wrong overall sign.
This is twice the correct minimum value.
This is half the correct minimum value.
This is off by three from the correct minimum value.
This is off by three from the correct minimum value.

stem: Use Lagrange multipliers to find the minimum value of \( f(x,y,z) = [[A]]x^{2} + [[B]]y^{2} + [[C]]z^{2} \) subject to the constraint \( [[p]]x + [[q]]y + [[r]]z = [[k]] \).
solution: The Lagrange conditions are \( 2[[A]]x=\lambda [[p]] \), \( 2[[B]]y=\lambda [[q]] \), \( 2[[C]]z=\lambda [[r]] \), together with the constraint \( [[p]]x+[[q]]y+[[r]]z=[[k]] \). Solving this system for \( x,y,z,\lambda \) and substituting back into \( f \) gives the minimum value \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the unconstrained critical value of \( f \) at the origin, ignoring the constraint entirely.
This ignores the \( z \)-direction entirely, as if the constraint and objective involved only \( x \) and \( y \).
This has the wrong overall sign.
This is twice the correct minimum value.
This is off by two from the correct minimum value.
This is off by two from the correct minimum value.
